attempts.test_code:
import signal
from source import sqrt


def test_sqrt_converges_to_correct_value():
    def _timeout(signum, frame):
        raise AssertionError(
            "sqrt(2, 0.01) did not terminate — buggy convergence check "
            "uses abs(x - approx) which never reaches epsilon because "
            "approx converges to ~1.414 while x=2, so abs(2 - 1.414) ≈ 0.586 > 0.01 forever"
        )

    signal.signal(signal.SIGALRM, _timeout)
    signal.alarm(3)
    try:
        result = sqrt(2, 0.01)
    finally:
        signal.alarm(0)

    assert abs(result - 1.41421356) < 0.01, (
        f"sqrt(2, 0.01) returned {result}, expected a value within 0.01 of sqrt(2) ≈ 1.41421"
    )
